title: EKS Runtime Detection and Backup â€” GuardDuty, audit alarms, AWS Backup vault lock
description: An engineer is wiring up the EKS detection + DR lane: GuardDuty EKS Audit Logs + Runtime Monitoring, CloudWatch metric filters + alarms for secret-read and pods/exec, an AWS Backup vault (KMS-encrypted) with optional immutable vault lock. Target end state matches Hulumi's `EksRuntimeDetectionFoundation` (M5) + `EksBackupFoundation` (M5). Threats focus on detection gaps (Fargate runtime-monitoring limitation), backup deletion (privileged AWS principal + irreversibility), and unencrypted recovery points.
actors: External Attacker (post-compromise; objective: persist + cover tracks)
Compromised AWS principal with `backup:*` (could delete recovery points)
assets: AWS Backup vault (and its KMS key)

stride.name: Spoofed source on backup-delete API call
stride.description: An attacker with `backup:DeleteRecoveryPoint` and `backup:DeleteBackupVault` permissions can erase recovery points before exfil. Mitigation: vault lock (immutable, with the M5 operator-confirmation gate); IAM scoping for backup-management principals.

stride.name: Tampered audit-log JSON shape
stride.description: If the EKS audit log JSON shape drifts (e.g. EKS adds `objectRef.subresource` placement), the M5 metric-filter patterns silently miss `pods/exec` events. Mitigation: kind/EKS integration tests against a real cluster; alarm coverage tracking via Hulumi/Drift adapter (M6).

stride.name: Repudiation via Fargate runtime blindness
stride.description: GuardDuty Runtime Monitoring is unsupported on EKS-on-Fargate. A compromised Fargate pod has no kernel-level introspection. Mitigation: `EksRuntimeDetectionFoundation` exposes `runtimeMonitoringUnsupported: Output<true>` so operators see the gap; control-plane audit events remain the detection backstop.

stride.name: Information disclosure via unencrypted backup vault
stride.description: AWS Backup defaults to AWS-managed KMS keys. A vault encrypted with an AWS-managed key is readable by any AWS-managed-key-using principal in the account, defeating cross-account isolation. Mitigation: `EksBackupFoundation` requires an explicit `kmsKeyArn` (no AWS-managed default).

stride.name: Denial of recovery via deleted recovery points
stride.description: Without vault lock, a privileged attacker can delete recovery points faster than the team detects the breach. Mitigation: `enableImmutableVaultLock` + the 3-day `changeableForDays` window leaves a confirmation gate without making the lock instantly irreversible.

stride.name: Elevation via cross-tenant backup vault access
stride.description: A backup IAM role over-scoped to `backup:*` on `*` can read any vault. Mitigation: scope the role to the specific vault ARNs; the component does NOT validate the role for the consumer (limitation noted in the M5 completion summary).

abuseCases.title: Fargate runtime blindness â€” compromised Fargate pod has no kernel-level detection
abuseCases.description: Mitigation: M5 component exposes the unsupported flag; kind tests verify the warning fires.

abuseCases.title: Backup deletion before exfil detection
abuseCases.description: Mitigation: vault lock + the 72h confirmation window in M5.

abuseCases.title: Mass-secret-read evades detection without metric filter
abuseCases.description: Mitigation: the secret-read metric filter + alarm pair in `EksRuntimeDetectionFoundation`.
